description: Port keen-pbr (Lua) to C++20 as an always-running daemon with optional REST API, targeting OpenWRT and Keenetic routers. Selective routing for IP addresses, subnets, and domains with interface failover and health checks.

userStories.title: Create Meson build system and Conan dependency files
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the build system foundation so that all subsequent code can compile. Create conanfile.py with dependencies (libcurl, nlohmann_json, libnl, mbedtls, cpp-httplib optional), meson.build with size-optimized flags and feature toggles, and meson_options.txt with build options (with_api, firewall_backend).
acceptanceCriteria: conanfile.py exists with dependencies: libcurl/8.5.0, nlohmann_json/3.11.3, libnl/3.8.0, mbedtls/3.5.0, cpp-httplib/0.14.0 (optional)
conanfile.py uses PkgConfigDeps and MesonToolchain generators
meson.build exists with project name 'keen-pbr3', C++20, size optimization flags (-Os -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections --gc-sections, LTO)
meson.build has 'with_api' feature flag for optional REST API compilation
meson_options.txt defines options: with_api (boolean, default true), firewall_backend (combo: auto/iptables/nftables)
include/keen-pbr3/version.hpp exists with version macros

userStories.title: Create cross-compilation profiles and cross-files
userStories.description: As a developer, I need cross-compilation support for embedded targets (MIPS, ARM, AArch64, x86_64) on OpenWRT and Keenetic.
acceptanceCriteria: conan/profiles/base-embedded exists with shared settings for embedded targets
conan/profiles/ has profiles for: mips-be-openwrt, mips-le-openwrt, arm-openwrt, aarch64-openwrt, x86_64-openwrt, mips-le-keenetic
Each profile includes base-embedded and sets correct arch, compiler, and OS settings
meson/cross/ directory has .ini cross-files for each architecture
Cross-files specify correct toolchain binaries (gcc, g++, ar, strip, pkg-config)

userStories.title: Create Docker build infrastructure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Docker-based builds using OpenWRT SDK images so that cross-compilation requires zero local toolchain setup.
acceptanceCriteria: docker/Dockerfile.openwrt exists, based on OpenWRT SDK image
Dockerfile installs Conan 2.x, Meson, Ninja, and required build tools
docker/build.sh exists and is executable
build.sh accepts architecture argument (e.g., mipsel_24kc)
build.sh runs Conan install, Meson setup, and Meson compile inside container
build.sh outputs binary to dist/<arch>/keen-pbr3

userStories.title: Implement HTTP client wrapper
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a libcurl wrapper for downloading IP/domain lists from URLs with HTTPS support via mbedTLS.
acceptanceCriteria: src/http/http_client.hpp declares HttpClient class with download method
src/http/http_client.cpp implements HttpClient using libcurl
Supports HTTPS via mbedTLS backend
download() method accepts URL string, returns response body as string
Supports setting timeout and user-agent
Handles HTTP errors by throwing exceptions with status code info
Typecheck passes (meson.build updated to include this source)

userStories.title: Implement JSON configuration parser - outbounds and daemon sections
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to parse the JSON config file's 'daemon' and 'outbounds' sections using nlohmann_json.
acceptanceCriteria: src/config/config.hpp declares Config struct with DaemonConfig and outbound types (InterfaceOutbound, TableOutbound, BlackholeOutbound)
Uses std::variant<InterfaceOutbound, TableOutbound, BlackholeOutbound> for Outbound type
InterfaceOutbound has: tag, interface, optional gateway, optional ping_target, ping_interval (default 30s), ping_timeout (default 5s)
TableOutbound has: tag, table_id
BlackholeOutbound has: tag
DaemonConfig has: pid_file, list_update_interval (default 24h)
src/config/config.cpp implements JSON deserialization for these types
Throws descriptive exceptions on invalid config
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement JSON configuration parser - lists, route, and DNS sections
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to parse the remaining config sections: lists definitions, route rules, and DNS configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: Config struct extended with ListConfig (url, domains, ip_cidrs, file path), RouteConfig, and DnsConfig
ListConfig supports: url (optional), inline domains (optional), inline ip_cidrs (optional), file path (optional)
RouteRule has: list names, action as variant of single outbound tag / failover chain / skip action
RouteConfig has: rules vector, fallback outbound tag
DnsServer has: tag, address (IP/DoH URL/system/rcode://refused), optional detour
DnsConfig has: servers vector, dns rules (list -> server), fallback server tag
ApiConfig has: enabled flag, listen address
Full Config struct has all sections: daemon, api, outbounds, dns, route, lists
JSON deserialization works for complete config format from PLAN.md
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement IP/domain list parser
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to parse text files containing IP addresses, CIDR subnets, and domain names (one per line).
acceptanceCriteria: src/config/list_parser.hpp declares ListParser with parse method
src/config/list_parser.cpp implements parsing of mixed IP/domain lists
Correctly identifies and parses: IPv4 addresses, IPv4 CIDR notation, IPv6 addresses, IPv6 CIDR notation, domain names, wildcard domains (*.example.com)
Skips empty lines and comment lines (starting with #)
Returns a struct with separate vectors for IPs, CIDRs, and domains
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement in-memory IP/subnet set
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an efficient in-memory data structure to check if an IP address matches any stored IP or CIDR subnet.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lists/ipset.hpp declares IpSet class
src/lists/ipset.cpp implements IpSet with add/contains operations
Supports adding individual IPv4/IPv6 addresses
Supports adding IPv4/IPv6 CIDR subnets
contains() method checks if an IP matches any stored address or falls within any stored subnet
Uses efficient lookup (not linear scan for every check)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement list manager for downloading and caching lists
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a list manager that downloads remote lists, reads local files, merges inline entries, caches results, and supports periodic refresh.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lists/list_manager.hpp declares ListManager class
src/lists/list_manager.cpp implements list loading from: URLs (via HttpClient), local files, inline domains/ip_cidrs
Merges all sources for a single list definition into one combined result
Caches downloaded lists to disk for offline startup
Provides a reload() method to re-download and refresh all lists
Returns parsed list data (IPs, CIDRs, domains) per list name
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement routing target type definitions

Defines SkipAction type for rules that should be ignored

userStories.title: Implement netlink route and rule management

userStories.title: Implement abstract route table and policy rule management

src/routing/route_table.cpp implements add/remove/clear operations for routes
Tracks currently installed routes to avoid duplicates and enable cleanup

src/routing/policy_rule.cpp implements add/remove/clear for policy rules
Both use NetlinkManager for actual kernel operations
Provides cleanup method to remove all installed routes/rules on shutdown

userStories.title: Implement health checker with ping-based monitoring

src/health/health_checker.cpp implements ping-based health checking

Respects ping_timeout from outbound config
Returns health status (healthy/unhealthy) per interface

userStories.title: Implement circuit breaker for failover stability
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a circuit breaker to prevent rapid failover oscillation between interfaces.
acceptanceCriteria: src/health/circuit_breaker.hpp declares CircuitBreaker class
src/health/circuit_breaker.cpp implements failure tracking per outbound
States: closed (healthy), open (failed, blocking), half-open (testing recovery)
Configurable failure threshold before opening circuit
Configurable cooldown period before attempting recovery (half-open)
Records success/failure and transitions states accordingly
Typecheck passes

Includes factory function to detect and create appropriate backend (iptables vs nftables)

userStories.title: Create ListEntryVisitor streaming interface and streaming ListParser
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a callback-based visitor pattern for processing list entries one-by-one without storing them in memory, and a streaming parser that reads from std::istream line-by-line.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lists/list_entry_visitor.hpp declares EntryType enum (Ip, Cidr, Domain) and ListEntryVisitor abstract class with on_entry(), on_list_complete(), finish() virtual methods
Includes FunctionalVisitor convenience class wrapping a std::function callback
Includes EntryCounter visitor class that counts ips/cidrs/domains without storing entries
src/config/list_parser.hpp adds static stream_parse(std::istream&, ListEntryVisitor&) and classify_entry(std::string_view, ListEntryVisitor&) methods
src/config/list_parser.cpp implements stream_parse() reading line-by-line with std::getline and dispatching to visitor via classify_entry()
classify_entry() reuses existing is_ipv4/is_cidr_v4/is_ipv6/is_cidr_v6/is_domain classification methods
ParsedList struct and parse() method remain temporarily for backward compatibility
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add configurable cache_dir to DaemonConfig and remove list_update_interval
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the cache directory to be configurable in the JSON config instead of hardcoded, and remove periodic list update interval since downloading is now a separate command.
acceptanceCriteria: DaemonConfig in src/config/config.hpp has cache_dir field with default '/var/cache/keen-pbr3'
list_update_interval field removed from DaemonConfig
src/config/config.cpp parses 'cache_dir' from daemon JSON section
config.example.json updated to show cache_dir option and remove list_update_interval
All references to hardcoded '/var/cache/keen-pbr3' in src/main.cpp replaced with config.daemon.cache_dir
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add conditional HTTP downloads with ETag and If-Modified-Since
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the HTTP client to support conditional downloads using ETag and If-Modified-Since headers to avoid re-downloading unchanged lists.
acceptanceCriteria: src/http/http_client.hpp declares ConditionalDownloadResult struct with fields: not_modified (bool), body (string), etag (string), last_modified (string)
HttpClient has new method download_conditional(url, if_none_match, if_modified_since) returning ConditionalDownloadResult
Implementation uses curl_slist_append to set If-None-Match and If-Modified-Since request headers
Implementation uses a header callback to capture ETag and Last-Modified from response headers
HTTP 304 Not Modified is handled as not_modified=true with empty body
Existing download() method remains unchanged for backward compatibility
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement CacheManager with metadata storage
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a CacheManager that handles downloading lists to cache files with ETag/If-Modified-Since support and stores metadata in .meta.json files.
acceptanceCriteria: src/cache/cache_manager.hpp declares CacheManager class with methods: ensure_dir(), download(name, url), has_cache(name), cache_path(name), meta_path(name), load_metadata(name), save_metadata(name, meta)
CacheMetadata struct has fields: etag, last_modified, url, download_time, and optional counts (ips, cidrs, domains)
src/cache/cache_manager.cpp implements download() using HttpClient::download_conditional() with stored ETag/Last-Modified from previous metadata
Cache files stored as <cache_dir>/<name>.txt, metadata as <cache_dir>/<name>.meta.json
download() returns true if content was updated, false if 304 Not Modified
On download failure, does not overwrite existing cache (falls back gracefully)
Metadata serialized/deserialized using nlohmann_json
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement ListStreamer for streaming from cache and inline entries
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a ListStreamer that replaces ListManager by streaming list entries from cache files and inline config through a visitor without storing them in memory.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lists/list_streamer.hpp declares ListStreamer class with methods: stream_list(name, ListConfig, visitor), stream_cache(name, visitor)
src/lists/list_streamer.cpp implements stream_list() which sequentially streams: cached URL file, local file (config.file), inline ip_cidrs via classify_entry(), inline domains as Domain entries
stream_file() opens file as std::ifstream and calls ListParser::stream_parse()
Calls visitor.on_list_complete(name) after all sources for a list are processed
Does not store any list entries in memory
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement IpsetRestoreVisitor for batch ipset loading
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a visitor that pipes IP/CIDR entries to 'ipset restore -exist' via popen to avoid one system() call per entry.
acceptanceCriteria: src/firewall/ipset_restore_pipe.hpp declares IpsetRestoreVisitor extending ListEntryVisitor
Constructor takes set_name and static_timeout, opens popen('ipset restore -exist', 'w')
on_entry() writes 'add <setname> <entry> [timeout N]\n' for Ip and Cidr types, ignores Domain entries
finish() calls pclose() and throws FirewallError if exit code is non-zero
Destructor calls finish() if not already called
count() returns number of entries written
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement NftBatchVisitor for batch nft set loading
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a visitor that pipes nftables element additions to 'nft -f -' via popen for batch loading.
acceptanceCriteria: src/firewall/nft_batch_pipe.hpp declares NftBatchVisitor extending ListEntryVisitor
Constructor takes set_name and static_timeout, opens popen('nft -f -', 'w')
on_entry() writes 'add element inet keen_pbr3 <setname> { <entry> [timeout Ns] }\n' for Ip and Cidr types, ignores Domain entries
finish() calls pclose() and throws FirewallError if exit code is non-zero
Destructor calls finish() if not already called
count() returns number of entries written
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add create_batch_loader() and flush_ipset() to Firewall interface and backends

src/firewall/iptables.hpp and .cpp implement create_batch_loader() returning IpsetRestoreVisitor
src/firewall/iptables.cpp implements flush_ipset() executing 'ipset flush <name>'
src/firewall/nftables.hpp and .cpp implement create_batch_loader() returning NftBatchVisitor
src/firewall/nftables.cpp implements flush_ipset() executing 'nft flush set inet keen_pbr3 <name>'

userStories.title: Rewrite daemon mode to use streaming firewall population and new signal semantics
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the daemon mode in main.cpp to use batch loaders for ipset population, SIGUSR1 for outbound re-selection, SIGHUP for full reload, and no periodic list update timer.
acceptanceCriteria: Daemon firewall loop uses firewall->create_batch_loader() + ListStreamer::stream_list() instead of iterating ParsedList vectors
Batch loader's finish() called after streaming each list
SIGUSR1 handler re-evaluates resolve_route_action() for failover routes using cached circuit breaker state, updates routes via 'ip route replace' if outbound changed
SIGHUP handler does full teardown (route_table.clear(), policy_rules.clear(), firewall->cleanup()) then full rebuild (re-read config, download if not cached, re-stream all lists, re-apply routing)
Periodic list update scheduler removed (no schedule_repeating for list refresh)
Health check periodic scheduler remains for interface outbounds
No ParsedList or ListManager used in daemon mode

userStories.title: Add fwmark and iproute configuration, assign marks to routable outbounds
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a new 'fwmark' config section that controls how firewall marks are allocated, and an 'iproute' section for routing table allocation. Each routable outbound (interface, table) gets a unique fwmark. Blackhole outbounds do NOT get a mark — they are handled by a direct DROP rule in the firewall. These marks become the universal abstraction for outbound selection.
acceptanceCriteria: FwmarkConfig struct added to config.hpp with fields: start (uint32_t, default 0x00010000), mask (uint32_t, default 0x00FF0000)
IprouteConfig struct added to config.hpp with field: table_start (uint32_t, default 150)
Config struct extended with fwmark (FwmarkConfig) and iproute (IprouteConfig) fields
JSON parsing implemented for 'fwmark' section (fields: 'start', 'mask') and 'iproute' section (field: 'table_start')

fwmark.mask validation: must have exactly two adjacent hex nibbles set to F (contiguous), e.g. 0x00FF0000 and 0x0000FF00 are valid, 0x0F0F0000 and 0x000F0F00 are not; throw ConfigError with descriptive message if invalid

Helper function allocate_outbound_marks() assigns sequential fwmarks to interface and table outbounds only (NOT blackhole, ignore, or urltest): first gets fwmark.start, second gets fwmark.start + step where step = (1 shifted by position of lowest set bit of mask), etc.
allocate_outbound_marks() validates that the number of routable outbounds does not exceed available mark space (256 for two F nibbles); throws ConfigError if exceeded
OutboundMarkMap type alias: std::map<std::string, uint32_t> mapping outbound tag to assigned fwmark
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement HTTP URL tester for urltest outbounds

Uses CURLOPT_MARK to set socket mark = fwmark, so kernel routes test traffic through the correct outbound's routing table via existing ip rules
Measures response time using std::chrono::steady_clock around the HTTP request
Implements retry logic: up to retry.attempts times with retry.interval_ms delay between attempts
Returns URLTestResult with success=true if HTTP status 200-299, latency_ms from fastest successful attempt

userStories.title: Implement urltest outbound selection manager
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a UrltestManager that runs periodic URL tests for all urltest outbounds, tracks per-child-outbound latencies and circuit breaker states, and selects the best outbound using the weighted group algorithm.
acceptanceCriteria: src/routing/urltest_manager.hpp declares UrltestManager class
UrltestState struct per urltest outbound with: last_results (map<string, URLTestResult>), circuit_breakers (map<string, CircuitBreaker>), selected_outbound (string)

register_urltest(UrltestOutbound) method: creates UrltestState, schedules repeating test at interval_ms
run_tests(tag) method: for each child outbound in the urltest, calls URLTester::test() using fwmark from OutboundMarkMap, updates circuit breakers, then calls select_outbound()

select_outbound(tag) implements: sort groups by weight ascending, for each group filter by circuit_breaker.is_allowed(), find fastest with tolerance (latency <= min_latency + tolerance_ms), return first found; if all exhausted return empty string (blackhole fallback)
When selected_outbound changes, invokes change callback with (urltest_tag, new_child_tag)
get_selected(urltest_tag) returns current selected child outbound tag
get_state(urltest_tag) returns const UrltestState& for API/status reporting
trigger_immediate_test(urltest_tag) runs tests immediately outside scheduled interval (for SIGUSR1)

userStories.title: Rewrite daemon mode with mark-based routing, stateful firewall, and transactional rebuilds
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to rewrite the daemon mode in main.cpp to use the new mark-based architecture: static routing tables/ip-rules set up once at startup, transactional firewall rule rebuilds when urltest selection changes, blackhole handled by firewall DROP rules, in-memory FirewallState tracking, and SIGUSR1 to verify routing tables and trigger immediate URL tests.
acceptanceCriteria: Startup: allocate_outbound_marks() assigns fwmarks to all interface/table outbounds

Startup: for each InterfaceOutbound, create a dedicated routing table (iproute.table_start + N) with a default route via its interface/gateway, add ip rule (fwmark/mask → table). These are static and never modified during runtime
Startup: for each TableOutbound, add ip rule (fwmark/mask → table_id). Static, never modified
BlackholeOutbound: no routing table, no ip rule, no fwmark — handled entirely by firewall DROP rule in apply_firewall()
apply_firewall() lambda replaces apply_all(): builds complete firewall ruleset transactionally, updates FirewallState

apply_firewall() for each route rule: if outbound is ignore → skip (no ipset, no rule); if outbound is blackhole → create ipset + DROP rule; if outbound is interface/table → create ipset + mark rule with fwmark; if outbound is urltest → resolve via UrltestManager to selected child, apply child's mark or DROP
apply_firewall() uses firewall->apply() for atomic application
apply_firewall() updates FirewallState with current rule-to-outbound mappings
UrltestManager initialized with change callback that triggers apply_firewall()
HealthChecker and make_health_fn() removed entirely
SIGUSR1 handler: (1) verify all static routing tables/ip-rules still exist (re-add if missing), (2) trigger immediate URL tests for all urltest outbounds
SIGHUP handler: full teardown (routes, rules, firewall), re-read config, re-allocate marks, re-create routing tables, re-initialize UrltestManager, apply_firewall()
rule_outbound_tags tracking removed — replaced by FirewallState
Periodic ping health check scheduler removed (replaced by UrltestManager scheduling)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Routing health data structures, Firewall::backend(), and accessor methods
userStories.description:
As a developer, I need the shared data types and accessor plumbing that all subsequent routing-health stories depend on.

Architecture:
- New header `src/health/routing_health.hpp` defines all result types in namespace `keen_pbr3`:
  - `enum class CheckStatus { ok, missing, mismatch }`
  - `struct FirewallChainCheck { bool chain_present; bool prerouting_hook_present; std::string detail; }`
  - `struct FirewallRuleCheck { std::string set_name; std::string action; std::optional<uint32_t> expected_fwmark; std::optional<uint32_t> actual_fwmark; CheckStatus status; std::string detail; }`
  - `struct RouteTableCheck { uint32_t table_id; std::string outbound_tag; std::optional<std::string> expected_interface; std::optional<std::string> expected_gateway; bool table_exists; bool default_route_present; bool interface_matches; bool gateway_matches; CheckStatus status; std::string detail; }`
  - `struct PolicyRuleCheck { uint32_t fwmark; uint32_t fwmask; uint32_t expected_table; uint32_t priority; bool rule_present_v4; bool rule_present_v6; CheckStatus status; std::string detail; }`
  - `struct RoutingHealthReport { bool overall_ok; std::string firewall_backend; FirewallChainCheck firewall_chain; std::vector<FirewallRuleCheck> firewall_rules; std::vector<RouteTableCheck> route_tables; std::vector<PolicyRuleCheck> policy_rules; std::string error; }`
- `enum class FirewallBackend { iptables, nftables }` added to `src/firewall/firewall.hpp`
- Pure virtual `virtual FirewallBackend backend() const = 0` added to `Firewall` abstract class in `firewall.hpp`
- `IptablesFirewall::backend()` returns `FirewallBackend::iptables`
- `NftablesFirewall::backend()` returns `FirewallBackend::nftables`
- `const std::vector<RouteSpec>& get_routes() const` added to `RouteTable` in `src/routing/route_table.hpp`
- `const std::vector<RuleSpec>& get_rules() const` added to `PolicyRuleManager` in `src/routing/policy_rule.hpp`
acceptanceCriteria: src/health/routing_health.hpp exists with CheckStatus enum and all five structs as described

Firewall abstract class declares pure virtual backend() const returning FirewallBackend
IptablesFirewall::backend() returns FirewallBackend::iptables
NftablesFirewall::backend() returns FirewallBackend::nftables

userStories.title: FirewallVerifier abstract interface + IptablesFirewallVerifier with parse_iptables_save()
userStories.description:
As a developer, I need an injectable verification layer for the iptables backend that compares expected rules (from FirewallState) with actual kernel state (from iptables-save).

Architecture:
- New header `src/firewall/firewall_verifier.hpp`:
  - `using CommandRunner = std::function<std::string(const std::string& cmd)>` — injectable function that runs a shell command and returns its stdout as a string
  - `std::string run_command_capture(const std::string& cmd)` — default implementation using popen, declared in header, defined in `firewall_verifier.cpp`
  - Abstract class `FirewallVerifier` with pure virtual methods:
    - `virtual FirewallChainCheck verify_chain() = 0`
    - `virtual std::vector<FirewallRuleCheck> verify_rules(const std::vector<RuleState>& expected) = 0`
  - Factory: `std::unique_ptr<FirewallVerifier> create_firewall_verifier(FirewallBackend backend, CommandRunner runner = run_command_capture)`
- New header/source `src/firewall/iptables_verifier.hpp/.cpp`:
  - Parse structs (exposed in header for direct testing):
    - `struct ParsedIptablesRule { std::string set_name; bool is_mark; bool is_drop; uint32_t fwmark; }`
    - `struct ParsedIptablesState { bool has_keen_pbr_chain; bool has_prerouting_jump; std::vector<ParsedIptablesRule> rules; }`
  - Pure function: `ParsedIptablesState parse_iptables_save(const std::string& output, bool ipv6 = false)` — scans lines looking for `:KeenPbrTable`, `-A PREROUTING -j KeenPbrTable`, and `-A KeenPbrTable` rules with `--match-set` + `MARK`/`DROP`
  - `class IptablesFirewallVerifier : public FirewallVerifier` with private `CommandRunner runner_`
  - `verify_chain()`: calls `runner_("iptables-save -t mangle")` and `runner_("ip6tables-save -t mangle")`, passes both to `parse_iptables_save()`, returns `FirewallChainCheck`
  - `verify_rules()`: parses both v4/v6 state, for each expected `RuleState` checks if a matching rule exists in parsed output (matching set_name, action, fwmark), returns vector of `FirewallRuleCheck` with status ok/missing/mismatch
acceptanceCriteria: src/firewall/firewall_verifier.hpp defines CommandRunner type alias, run_command_capture() declaration, FirewallVerifier abstract class, and create_firewall_verifier() factory
src/firewall/firewall_verifier.cpp implements run_command_capture() using popen; factory returns IptablesFirewallVerifier or NftablesFirewallVerifier based on backend arg
src/firewall/iptables_verifier.hpp exposes ParsedIptablesRule, ParsedIptablesState, and parse_iptables_save() as public symbols
parse_iptables_save() correctly identifies chain presence, prerouting jump, mark rules (with fwmark value), and drop rules from iptables-save -t mangle output
IptablesFirewallVerifier::verify_chain() invokes CommandRunner for both iptables-save and ip6tables-save and aggregates results
IptablesFirewallVerifier::verify_rules() returns FirewallRuleCheck per expected rule with correct status (ok/missing/mismatch) and actual_fwmark populated when found
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: NftablesFirewallVerifier with parse_nft_json()
userStories.description:
As a developer, I need the nftables backend verifier that reads `nft -j list ruleset` JSON output and compares the KeenPbrTable inet table/chain/rules against expected state.

Architecture:
- New header/source `src/firewall/nftables_verifier.hpp/.cpp`:
  - Parse structs (exposed in header for direct testing):
    - `struct ParsedNftRule { std::string set_name; bool is_mark; bool is_drop; uint32_t fwmark; bool ipv6; }`
    - `struct ParsedNftablesState { bool has_table; bool has_prerouting_chain; bool has_prerouting_hook; std::vector<ParsedNftRule> rules; }`
  - Pure function: `ParsedNftablesState parse_nft_json(const std::string& json_output)` using nlohmann_json:
    - Walks `nftables` array from `nft -j list ruleset` output
    - Finds element with key `"table"` where `family == "inet"` and `name == "KeenPbrTable"`
    - Finds element with key `"chain"` in that table named `"prerouting"`; checks `hook == "prerouting"` and `type == "filter"`
    - Finds elements with key `"rule"` in that chain; for each rule walks `expr` array:
      - Mark rule: contains `match` with `right` referencing `@setname` + `mangle` expr with mark value
      - Drop rule: contains `match` with `right` referencing `@setname` + `"drop"` expr
    - Determines ipv6 from set name suffix or set type if available
  - `class NftablesFirewallVerifier : public FirewallVerifier` with private `CommandRunner runner_`
  - `verify_chain()`: calls `runner_("nft -j list ruleset")`, passes to `parse_nft_json()`, returns `FirewallChainCheck`
  - `verify_rules()`: for each expected `RuleState` checks if matching rule exists in parsed state, returns vector of `FirewallRuleCheck`
- `create_firewall_verifier()` in `firewall_verifier.cpp` returns `NftablesFirewallVerifier` when backend is `FirewallBackend::nftables`
acceptanceCriteria: src/firewall/nftables_verifier.hpp exposes ParsedNftRule, ParsedNftablesState, and parse_nft_json() as public symbols
parse_nft_json() correctly identifies table presence, chain presence, prerouting hook presence, mark rules (with fwmark), and drop rules from nft -j list ruleset JSON
parse_nft_json() returns has_table=false and empty rules when table KeenPbrTable is not found
parse_nft_json() returns has_prerouting_hook=false when chain exists but hook is not configured
NftablesFirewallVerifier::verify_chain() invokes CommandRunner for nft -j list ruleset
NftablesFirewallVerifier::verify_rules() returns correct status (ok/missing/mismatch) per expected rule
nlohmann_json used for JSON parsing (already available as submodule)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: NetlinkManager dump methods + RoutingVerifier
userStories.description:
As a developer, I need to verify actual routing tables and policy rules from the kernel using netlink (libnl3), not CLI tools.

Architecture:
- Add to `src/routing/netlink.hpp` two new structs and two new dump methods on `NetlinkManager`:
  - `struct DumpedRoute { std::string destination; uint32_t table; std::optional<std::string> interface; std::optional<std::string> gateway; bool blackhole; int family; }`
  - `struct DumpedRule { uint32_t priority; uint32_t fwmark; uint32_t fwmask; uint32_t table; int family; }`
  - `std::vector<DumpedRoute> dump_routes_in_table(uint32_t table_id, int family = AF_UNSPEC)` — uses `rtnl_route_alloc_cache(sock_, family, 0, &cache)` + `nl_cache_foreach()` to iterate, filters by `rtnl_route_get_table() == table_id`; extracts destination via `rtnl_route_get_dst()`, interface index via `rtnl_route_get_oif()` → `rtnl_link_i2name()`, gateway via next-hop `rtnl_route_nh_get_gateway()`
  - `std::vector<DumpedRule> dump_policy_rules(int family = AF_UNSPEC)` — uses `rtnl_rule_alloc_cache(sock_, family, &cache)` + `nl_cache_foreach()`; extracts priority/fwmark/fwmask/table via `rtnl_rule_get_*()` functions
- Implement both in `src/routing/netlink.cpp`
- New header/source `src/routing/routing_verifier.hpp/.cpp`:
  - `class RoutingVerifier` with `explicit RoutingVerifier(NetlinkManager& netlink)`
  - `RouteTableCheck verify_route_table(const RouteSpec& expected, const std::string& outbound_tag)`: calls `netlink_.dump_routes_in_table(spec.table)`, looks for a default route (`destination == "default"` or prefix length 0), compares interface and gateway to expected values, fills `RouteTableCheck`
  - `PolicyRuleCheck verify_policy_rule(const RuleSpec& expected, const std::string& outbound_tag)`: calls `netlink_.dump_policy_rules()`, checks that a rule with matching fwmark+fwmask+table exists for AF_INET and AF_INET6, fills `PolicyRuleCheck`
acceptanceCriteria: DumpedRoute and DumpedRule structs declared in src/routing/netlink.hpp
NetlinkManager::dump_routes_in_table() implemented using rtnl_route_alloc_cache + nl_cache_foreach, returns routes filtered by table_id
NetlinkManager::dump_policy_rules() implemented using rtnl_rule_alloc_cache + nl_cache_foreach
dump_routes_in_table() correctly populates interface name (resolved from ifindex) and gateway string
src/routing/routing_verifier.hpp/.cpp exist with RoutingVerifier class taking NetlinkManager& in constructor
verify_route_table() returns RouteTableCheck with correct table_exists, default_route_present, interface_matches, gateway_matches, and status fields
verify_policy_rule() returns PolicyRuleCheck with correct rule_present_v4, rule_present_v6, and status fields
No popen/system/CLI calls in RoutingVerifier or NetlinkManager dump methods — only libnl3 APIs
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: RoutingHealthChecker orchestrator + JSON serialization
userStories.description:
As a developer, I need the RoutingHealthChecker to tie together the firewall and routing verifiers and produce a serializable RoutingHealthReport.

Architecture:
- New header/source `src/health/routing_health_checker.hpp/.cpp`:
  - `class RoutingHealthChecker` constructor: `RoutingHealthChecker(const Firewall& firewall, const FirewallState& firewall_state, const RouteTable& route_table, const PolicyRuleManager& policy_rules, NetlinkManager& netlink)`
  - `RoutingHealthReport check()` method:
    1. Calls `create_firewall_verifier(firewall_.backend())` to get the right verifier
    2. Calls `verifier->verify_chain()` → populates `report.firewall_chain`
    3. Gets expected rules from `firewall_state_.get_rules()` (vector of RuleState); calls `verifier->verify_rules(expected_rules)` → populates `report.firewall_rules`
    4. Creates `RoutingVerifier rv(netlink_)`
    5. Iterates `route_table_.get_routes()` → calls `rv.verify_route_table(spec, tag)` per entry → populates `report.route_tables`
    6. Iterates `policy_rules_.get_rules()` → calls `rv.verify_policy_rule(spec, tag)` per entry → populates `report.policy_rules`
    7. Sets `report.overall_ok = true` only if all checks have `CheckStatus::ok`
    8. Sets `report.firewall_backend` to `"iptables"` or `"nftables"`
    9. Wraps entire body in try/catch; on exception sets `report.error` and `report.overall_ok = false`
  - Free function `nlohmann::json routing_health_report_to_json(const RoutingHealthReport& r)` — serializes to JSON matching the response schema:
    - `"overall"`: `"ok"` / `"degraded"` / `"error"`
    - `"firewall_backend"`, `"firewall"` object, `"firewall_rules"` array, `"route_tables"` array, `"policy_rules"` array
    - fwmark/fwmask values formatted as hex strings (e.g. `"0x00010000"`)
    - CheckStatus serialized as `"ok"` / `"missing"` / `"mismatch"` strings
acceptanceCriteria: src/health/routing_health_checker.hpp/.cpp exist with RoutingHealthChecker class
RoutingHealthChecker constructor accepts Firewall const ref, FirewallState const ref, RouteTable const ref, PolicyRuleManager const ref, NetlinkManager ref
check() calls create_firewall_verifier() with backend from firewall_.backend()
check() aggregates results from FirewallVerifier and RoutingVerifier into RoutingHealthReport
overall_ok is true only when all sub-checks have status CheckStatus::ok
check() catches exceptions and populates report.error; overall_ok is false on error
routing_health_report_to_json() produces JSON matching the documented response schema
fwmark values appear as hex strings in JSON output
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Wire GET /api/health/routing endpoint into API and Daemon
userStories.description:
As a developer, I need the new routing health endpoint exposed via the REST API so clients can query it.

Architecture:
- `src/api/handlers.hpp` — add `RoutingHealthChecker& routing_health_checker` field to `ApiContext` struct
- `src/api/handlers.cpp` — register handler:
  ```
  GET /api/health/routing → ctx.routing_health_checker.check() → routing_health_report_to_json() → 200 response
  ```
  On any exception caught at handler boundary: respond with `{"overall":"error","error":"<message>"}` and HTTP 500
- `src/daemon/daemon.hpp` — add `std::unique_ptr<RoutingHealthChecker> routing_health_checker_` member (inside `#ifdef WITH_API` or unconditionally, as it's lightweight)
- `src/daemon/daemon.cpp`:
  - After firewall, firewall_state, route_table, policy_rules, and netlink are constructed, construct: `routing_health_checker_ = std::make_unique<RoutingHealthChecker>(*firewall_, firewall_state_, route_table_, policy_rules_, *netlink_manager_)`
  - In `setup_api()`: pass `*routing_health_checker_` to `ApiContext`
- `CMakeLists.txt` — add all new source files to the SOURCES list:
  - `src/health/routing_health_checker.cpp`
  - `src/firewall/firewall_verifier.cpp`
  - `src/firewall/iptables_verifier.cpp`
  - `src/firewall/nftables_verifier.cpp`
  - `src/routing/routing_verifier.cpp`
acceptanceCriteria: ApiContext in handlers.hpp has routing_health_checker field of type RoutingHealthChecker&
GET /api/health/routing is registered in handlers.cpp and calls routing_health_checker.check()
Handler returns 200 with JSON body on success
Handler returns 500 with error JSON on exception
Daemon constructs RoutingHealthChecker after all its dependencies are initialized
RoutingHealthChecker passed to ApiContext in setup_api()
All new .cpp files added to CMakeLists.txt SOURCES
curl http://127.0.0.1:PORT/api/health/routing returns valid JSON with overall, firewall_backend, firewall, firewall_rules, route_tables, policy_rules fields
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Unit tests for firewall parsing functions using doctest
userStories.description:
As a developer, I need unit tests for the pure parse functions (parse_iptables_save, parse_nft_json) and the CommandRunner-injectable verifier classes so correctness can be verified without a running kernel.

Architecture:
- Add `doctest` as a git submodule at `third_party/doctest/` (single header: `third_party/doctest/doctest/doctest.h`), following the same pattern as nlohmann_json and cpp-httplib submodules
- `.gitmodules` gets a new entry pointing to `https://github.com/doctest/doctest.git`
- New test file `tests/test_firewall_verifier.cpp`:
  - `#define DOCTEST_CONFIG_IMPLEMENT_WITH_MAIN` + `#include <doctest/doctest.h>`
  - Tests for `parse_iptables_save()`:
    - Empty string → has_keen_pbr_chain=false, has_prerouting_jump=false, empty rules
    - Chain declaration present but no jump → has_keen_pbr_chain=true, has_prerouting_jump=false
    - PREROUTING jump present → has_prerouting_jump=true
    - Mark rule with fwmark → rule parsed with is_mark=true, correct fwmark value
    - Drop rule → rule parsed with is_drop=true
    - Multiple rules → all parsed in order
    - IPv6 variant (ip6tables-save output) → same results
  - Tests for `parse_nft_json()`:
    - Invalid JSON → has_table=false, no exception
    - Valid JSON with no KeenPbrTable table → has_table=false
    - Table present, no chain → has_table=true, has_prerouting_chain=false
    - Chain present, no hook → has_prerouting_chain=true, has_prerouting_hook=false
    - Mark rule → parsed with correct fwmark
    - Drop rule → parsed with is_drop=true
    - Wrong table name → has_table=false
  - Tests for `IptablesFirewallVerifier::verify_rules()` with injected CommandRunner:
    - Inject lambda returning canned iptables-save string; pass expected RuleState; assert returned FirewallRuleCheck statuses
  - Tests for `NftablesFirewallVerifier::verify_rules()` with injected CommandRunner:
    - Same pattern with canned nft JSON string
- `CMakeLists.txt` additions:
  - `option(BUILD_TESTS "Build unit tests" OFF)`
  - When `BUILD_TESTS=ON`: add `tests/` subdirectory with `CMakeLists.txt` that creates `keen-pbr3-tests` executable linking `iptables_verifier`, `nftables_verifier`, `firewall_verifier` object files and doctest header
  - Routing verifier is NOT unit-tested (requires real kernel via libnl3); covered by integration/manual testing
acceptanceCriteria: third_party/doctest/ git submodule added with doctest/doctest.h header present
.gitmodules contains entry for doctest submodule
tests/test_firewall_verifier.cpp exists with doctest-based tests
parse_iptables_save() tests cover: empty input, chain only, prerouting jump, mark rule, drop rule, multiple rules, ipv6=true
parse_nft_json() tests cover: invalid JSON, missing table, missing chain, missing hook, mark rule with fwmark, drop rule
IptablesFirewallVerifier and NftablesFirewallVerifier tested with injected CommandRunner lambdas returning canned strings
cmake -DBUILD_TESTS=ON && cmake --build build && ./build/keen-pbr3-tests succeeds without root
Typecheck passes
